### Post by 449 on 2008-01-18
I think it's after I messed with Fluxkeys that my left mouse click decided to stop working. How do I fix this? 

```
# File generated by FluxConf
None Mouse1top :HideMenus
None Mouse2top :WorkspaceMenu
None Mouse3top :RootMenu
None Mouse4top :NextWorkspace
None Mouse5top :PrevWorkspace
Mod1 Tab :NextWindow
Mod1 Shift Tab :PrevWindow
Control Shift F1 :Quit
Mod1 F2 :Workspace 2
Mod1 F3 :Workspace 3
Mod1 F4 :Workspace 4
Mod1 F5 :Workspace 5
Mod1 F6 :Workspace 6
Mod1 F7 :Workspace 7
Mod1 F8 :Workspace 8
Mod1 F9 :Workspace 9
Mod1 F10 :Workspace 10
Mod1 F11 :Workspace 11
Mod1 F12 :Workspace 12
```

---

### Post by p_quarles on 2008-01-18
Yes, Fluxkeys is the culprit. I've filed a bug report on it already, but I doubt it'll get much attention. 

Change the mouse button entries to follow this format:
```
OnDesktop Mouse1 :hideMenus
```
I'd also recommend removing the Fluxkeys entry from your menu file -- even accidentally clicking on it will cause the same thing to happen. 

Btw, when this happens, you can still use the mouse buttons normally by holding down the left CTRL key while clicking.

---

### Post by cknight on 2008-01-18
You can also check out this thread on fluxbox keys file editing.  You'll have to do it yourself without a GUI (since there isn't really a usable GUI keys editor out there), but its not really hard.  

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=617812]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=617812")

Its sad, but I wonder how many people give up on Fluxbox because of Fluxkeys?
